source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 1. 2차 CE 인허가/Attachment 08. Software Validation Report/8-2 Software IEC 62304 checklist (A-TCF-5-047).xlsx

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 62304 checklist for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software validation, revised on 2021.04.02.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document applicability and supporting evidence for IEC 62304 medical device software lifecycle, risk management, configuration management, maintenance, and problem resolution processes.
keyPoints: Product Name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and Model Name is ADD.
Documentation No. is A-TCF-5-047 with Rev. No. 1 and Rev. Date 2021.04.02.

The checklist maps IEC 62304 clauses to supporting document A-TCF-5-046 sections including software lifecycle, requirements, architecture, verification and validation, hazard analysis, and revision history.
Risk management process compliance references ISO 14971.
Several SOUP-related items are marked Not applicable.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product Name,,,Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people resulting from a HAZARD

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

concepts.name: Software verification and validation testing
concepts.evidence: 5.1.6,Software VERIFICATION planning task,X,X,X,A-TCF-5-046,9. Software Verification and Validation Test

concepts.name: SOUP
concepts.evidence: If a SOFTWARE ITEM is identified as SOUP, the MANUFACTURER has specified functional and performance requirements for the SOUP item that are necessary for its intended use.

evidence.claim: Software safety classes A, B and C are assigned according to possible effects on patient, operator or other people.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people resulting from a HAZARD

evidence.claim: Class A means no injury or damage to health is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: No injury or damage to health is possible

evidence.claim: Class B means non-serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: Class C means death or serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class C: Death or SERIOUS INJURY is possible

reviewFlags.reason: No authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal is only 'general'.
Source metadata productSignal is 'HAD/A/F 1417-1717', but the document text states Model Name 'ADD'.
The extracted spreadsheet text contains typographical or conversion artifacts such as 'PROCESESS', 'developmen', and 'Software Lift Cycle'.
